text: Hello! Today I will be doing a Valentino Spring 2012 inspired hair tutorial, which is pretty
much a wrap around braid around your head. First I will start out with my normal texturized
hair. I will start at the top of the center of my head and there I will start my French
braid. I will split that into three and start French braiding at a right angle towards my
right ear. Therefore it is kind of like a right diagonal. With this braid you don't
want to take a lot from the outside of your head because you also have a French braid
surrounding your head. So just keep it simple and light and keep braiding at the right angle.
Ok, once you start to reach your ear turn the braid up going towards your forehead like
so. Around the forehead I like to take more hair strands from the front just to have an
obvious braid look.
Continue that braid all the way around your forehead.
The hardest part is probably braiding the back side of your head but it takes time and
practice and you can get it.
Ok, one tip is to braid tightly around your head because the braid can easily fall out
if you don't braid securely enough.
At the end of the braid you want to braid at an upward angle because you will be placing
the loose braid behind the first braid that you already braided.
I will secure the end with two elastics for extra security.
And then I will place the loose braid right behind the French braid that is surrounding
my head.
Ok, taking the loose braid that I will call braid 2 and then the braid 1 will be the one
I already made around my forehead. I will place braid 2 behind braid 1 and bobby pin
that loose braid securely to my head and I will also tuck the extra hair sticking out
of braid 2 under braid 1.
And then bobby pin away. Go crazy and make sure that loose braid won't fall out throughout
the day.
And there you have it, a simple cute French head braid that will keep your hair off your
shoulders on the hot summer days.
Thank you for watching this tutorial. Feel free to subscribe and like this video. I
appreciate all your views and I hope you can try this out in the future.
Bye bye!
